skill.name: caveman-review
skill.description: Formats code review feedback as short, actionable pull request comments with exact locations, clear problems, and concrete fixes.
skill.summary: Turn review feedback into concise PR comments that keep the actionable signal.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell execution, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but each is a false positive in context. SKILL.md contains only markdown formatting guidance and review examples, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged lines use markdown inline code formatting for review comment examples and labels. They do not define scripts or execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is markdown prose and review comment examples. No command runner, script block, or executable instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Library Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes invoking the skill with /caveman-review and auto-triggering for pull request reviews. No HTTP client or network request appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 7 is plain skill metadata text. I found no evidence of Python HTTP libraries or outbound network behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the YAML description field for the skill. It does not mention or implement a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 starts a multiline description in skill metadata. I found no evidence of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic code.

content.value_statement: Pull request feedback often becomes too long to act on quickly. This skill converts review notes into direct comments with location, problem, and fix.
actual_capabilities: Formats each review finding as a single actionable line.
Includes exact line numbers and symbols when available.
Adds optional severity prefixes for bugs, risks, nits, and questions.
Removes hedging, repeated context, and broad praise from comments.
Switches to fuller explanations for security or architecture findings.
limitations: Does not inspect code without a provided diff or review context.
Does not run linters, tests, or security tools.
Does not write code fixes for the author.
Does not approve or request changes on a pull request.

use_cases.title: Compress PR Feedback
use_cases.description: Turn long review notes into short comments that authors can act on immediately.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reviews
use_cases.description: Keep review comments consistent across reviewers by using the same compact format.

use_cases.title: Prepare Review Summaries
use_cases.description: Convert rough observations into paste-ready comments before submitting a review.

prompt_templates.title: Review This Diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this diff with caveman-review. Use one line per finding with location, problem, and fix.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for concise review comments

prompt_templates.title: Triage Mixed Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request. Add severity prefixes only when findings include bugs, risks, nits, or questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Review with different levels of urgency

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Existing Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite these review notes in caveman-review style. Keep exact line references and concrete fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this architecture-heavy diff. Use normal paragraphs for major design concerns, then terse comments for local issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review where some findings need rationale

output_examples.input: A reviewer found a null access risk on line 42 after a lookup can return no user.
output_examples.output: L42: bug: user can be null after lookup. Add guard before reading email.

output_examples.input: A reviewer sees missing rate limit handling on line 23 after an API call.
output_examples.output: L23: risk: no handling for rate limit responses. Add retry with backoff.

output_examples.input: A reviewer wants a long helper split into clearer steps.
output_examples.output: L88: nit: helper mixes validation, normalization, and persistence. Split into named functions.

best_practices: Provide the diff and relevant line numbers before asking for review comments.
Use severity prefixes when comments mix defects, risks, nits, and questions.
Use normal explanation for security, architecture, or onboarding feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to replace evidence for serious security findings.
Do not ask it to approve or reject a pull request.
Do not remove important context when the author needs rationale.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It formats review feedback into short pull request comments with location, problem, and fix.

faq.question: Does it review code automatically?
faq.answer: It can format findings from a provided diff, but it does not run tools or tests.

faq.question: Can it handle security findings?
faq.answer: Yes, but it should use fuller explanations for serious security issues.

faq.question: Does it write code fixes?
faq.answer: No. It describes concrete fixes for the author to apply.

faq.question: Which tools support it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: When should I stop using terse mode?
faq.answer: Use a normal review style for architecture debates, onboarding, or findings that need detailed evidence.
